title: 'Look at your five fingers': international students and reproduction of class inequalities at university
abstract: This article looks at how institutional peer leadership roles in higher education reproduce social class inequalities. Through an ethnographic case study of Southern African Development Community (SADC) international students studying at Rhodes University in South Africa, I demonstrate how students from privileged class backgrounds possess the required social and cultural capital to succeed in these roles. Contrary to common perceptions of international students as a marginalised group, and despite the integration challenges that SADC students face as migrants in the wider South African society, I argue that their relative class advantage compared to many South African students temporarily negates their sense of foreignness. Analysing peer leadership as paid employment, I highlight the role of racialised work ethic in the reproduction of class inequalities in higher education.
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
